content.name: Bachelor Paper: Comparison of data exfiltration techniques for stealing Windows using keystroke injection attacks
content.content: This paper compares keystroke injection data exfiltration techniques using specifically developed DuckyScript payloads deployed using the Hak5 USB Rubber Ducky. In particular, it covers exfiltration over a physical medium (USB), exfiltration over a network medium (HTTP), as well as the novel exfiltration method known as keystroke reflection. It was written as the final thesis of my Bachelor's degree at UAS Technikum Wien.

content.name: Write-Up: TryHackMe Red Team Capstone Challenge
content.content: The TryHackMe Red Team Capstone Challenge simulated a red team assessment on the corporate network infrastructure of a fictional bank. The objective was to enumerate and exploit the Active Directory environment to conduct a fraudulent money transfer to show the compromise of the bank's network. This 40+ page long write-up details the steps taken to complete the challenge and includes techniques like Kerberoasting, DCSync attacks, Golden ticket attacks as well as lateral movement and persistence inside a Active Directory domain.
